Version notice:

F5 BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes¶

F5 BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes (BNK) brings critical network capabilities to a Kubernetes environment to meet the demands of service provider networks. BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes is deployed as a Kubernetes resource at the North/South gateway, becoming part of the Kubernetes orchestration environment. F5 Cloud Native Network Functions (CNFs) extend F5 BIG-IP solutions into the container ecosystem.

BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes is designed to be Kubernetes‑native. You can deploy it in IaaS environments by using Helm or the F5 Lifecycle Operator (FLO), and manage it with standard tools including Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) Gateway API. Designed following the Kubernetes Operator pattern, FLO manages BNK installation, upgrades, and rollbacks.

Visit the Overview page to learn more about BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes, its deployment models, and supported use cases.

About this website¶

Note

Documentation for the BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes Service Proxy and BIG-IP Cloud-Native Network functions is being consolidated here.

You can find the following documentation in this location:

  • Consolidated installation instructions for BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes (refer to Install)

  • Reference material, task instructions, and use cases for BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes.

You can find reference material, task instructions, and use cases for F5 Cloud-Native Network Functions (CNFs) at the following locations:

  • CNFs on AON

  • CNFs on OpenShift

  • CNFs on Robin
